name: security.scanSecrets
description: Execute the GuideAI secret scanning workflow (Gitleaks via pre-commit) and return structured findings for downstream remediation and action logging.

surface.description: Surface invoking the scan. Used for telemetry attribution and guardrail routing.

paths.description: Relative paths to include in the scan. Defaults to repository root.

fail_on_findings.description: Return a non-zero exit code when findings are detected so callers can block merges/deploys.

report_format.description: Preferred output style for findings. JSON is recommended for automation.

report_path.description: Optional path (relative or absolute) where the JSON report should be written for archive/audit purposes.

scan_id.description: Unique identifier for this scan execution.

status.description: Overall scan outcome. FAILED indicates at least one finding when fail_on_findings=true.

report_path.description: Filesystem location of the persisted JSON report when provided.

rule.description: Identifier of the detection rule triggered by the finding.

file.description: Path to the file containing the potential secret.

line.description: Line number where the secret was detected.

excerpt.description: Redacted snippet providing context for remediation.

entropy.description: Optional entropy score reported by the scanner.

findings.description: List of sanitized findings from the scan. Empty when status=PASSED.

metadata.description: Additional details such as scanner version, execution host, and command invocation.

errors.description: Pre-commit or the Gitleaks hook was not installed, preventing the scan from executing.
Underlying scan process returned an unexpected error code. Check metadata.command for context.
